full_text_rationale: Full text describes TextBFGS maintaining and retaining a dynamic case base of historical error-to-operator correction trajectories for code optimization, with HumanEval and MBPP evaluation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 013; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Zizheng Zhang, Yuyang Liao, Chen Chen, Jian He, Dun Wu, Qianjin Yu, Yanqin Gao, Jin Yang, Kailai Zhang, Eng Siong Chng, Xionghu Zhong
titulo: TextBFGS: A Case-Based Reasoning Approach to Code Optimization via Error-Operator Retrieval
formato_armazenamento: Dynamic Case Base of historical code-optimization trajectories stored in ChromaDB, with each case containing pre-optimization text, error feedback or gradient, abstract operator, and post-optimization text.
framework_agente: TextBFGS case-based reasoning optimizer with error-operator retrieval, one-pass update, and successful-case retention
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; in-domain and cross-domain case-base comparison; retrieval ablation; efficiency comparison
benchmarks_utilizados: HumanEval-Hard and MBPP-Hard filtered coding-optimization tasks
metricas_desempenho: Base Pass, Plus Pass, calls per task, tokens per call, tokens per task
metricas_cl: Case-base construction with 39 HumanEval trajectories and 245 MBPP trajectories; retrieval by error/gradient similarity; successful adaptations retained back into the case base.
baseline_comparado: TextGrad, TextGrad-Momentum, TextBFGS without case base, and TextBFGS-REMO with problem-based retrieval
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Case bases contain 39 HumanEval case trajectories and 245 MBPP trajectories in the reported setup; successful adaptations can be retained dynamically.
custo_por_tarefa: NR monetary cost; calls and token counts per task are reported.
reducao_tokens: HumanEval-Hard cross-domain TextBFGS uses 21.6k tokens/task versus 30.9k for TextGrad and 43.7k for TextGrad-Momentum; MBPP in-domain TextBFGS uses 17.2k tokens/task versus 26.6k for TextGrad.
resultado_principal: On HumanEval-Hard using the MBPP case base, TextBFGS reaches 97.78 Base Pass and 93.33 Plus Pass, exceeding TextBFGS without case base and REMO. On MBPP-Hard using the MBPP case base, TextBFGS reaches 94.02 Base Pass and 74.36 Plus Pass.
desempenho_vs_baseline: On HumanEval-Hard, TextBFGS reaches 97.78/93.33 versus 91.11/82.22 for TextBFGS without case base and 95.56/91.11 for REMO. Cross-domain TextBFGS maintains 74.36 MBPP Plus Pass using a HumanEval case base versus 58.12 for REMO.
custo_vs_baseline: Compared with TextGrad-Momentum on HumanEval-Hard, TextBFGS uses about 50.6% fewer tokens per task; the paper also reports lower calls per task than TextGrad/TextGrad-Momentum.
limitacoes: Evaluation focuses on filtered coding-optimization benchmarks and case trajectories from HumanEval/MBPP; generalization to larger repositories and richer optimization objectives is not established.
ameacas_validade: Filtered hard-task construction, case-base source domain, retrieval embedding design, baseline reimplementation choices, and maximum-iteration limits may affect validity.
